title: DETERMIN ATION OF URANIUM USING DISODIUM I : 8-DIHYDROXY. NAPHTHALHNE-3 :6-DISULPHONATE (CHROMOTROPIC SALT) AS A COLORIMETRIC REAGENT
author: ANIL. K. MUKHERJI
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The use of disodium I :S-dihydroxyna.phlhalene-3:6-disulphonate (chromotropic salt) as a reagent q,,r the determination of uranium on a micro-scale has been described. The reagent is sensitise to 30 p.p m. of uranium and Beer's law holds good in the range of concentration of 50-200 p.p m The reagent added should be at least 30 times the molar concentration of uranium. The  to  of the medium should be adjusted at 4.00 ± 0.50. For the determination, either a photoelectric colori­meter with a blue rater or a spectrophotometer can be employed. The interference of various cations and anions has also been studied. The chelate has a composition of I :I of the reactants.
